StackView.Name: HeroContent
StackView.TextBlock: A modern UI engine for games and native apps. Compose layouts from grids, stacks, text, and images — all rendered at 60 fps.

TextBlock.Name: TabbedTechHeading
TextBlock.Text: Orca's stack, in one place

TextBlock.Name: TabbedTechSubheading
TextBlock.Text: XML lays out the screen, Lua drives behavior, and MoonScript keeps larger app code concise while the same UI ships across platforms.

TextBlock.Name: XmlTitle
TextBlock.Text: XML keeps layout visible

TextBlock.Name: XmlBody
TextBlock.Text: The page reads top to bottom like a UI blueprint, so structure stays easy to inspect, diff, and evolve.

TextBlock.Name: XmlNote1
TextBlock.Text: • Grids and stacks map directly to visual structure.

TextBlock.Name: XmlNote2
TextBlock.Text: • Text, spacing, and padding stay explicit instead of buried in helper code.

TextBlock.Name: XmlNote3
TextBlock.Text: • You can open a screen and understand the layout without chasing another language.

TextBlock.Name: XmlModelHeading
TextBlock.Text: A simple mental model

TextBlock.Name: XmlModelLine2
TextBlock.Text: Grid

TextBlock.Name: XmlModelLine3
TextBlock.Text: StackView

TextBlock.Name: XmlModelLine4
TextBlock.Text: TabView

TextBlock.Name: XmlModelLine5
TextBlock.Text: ImageView

TextBlock.Name: XmlModelLine6
TextBlock.Text: TextBlock

TextBlock.Name: LuaTitle
TextBlock.Text: Lua stays close to the UI

TextBlock.Name: LuaBody
TextBlock.Text: Behavior, input, and data binding can sit right next to the screen that uses them, which makes iteration feel lightweight.

LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Name: LuaCard1
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Body: Wire up taps, clicks, and state changes without building a separate framework around the screen.

LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Name: LuaCard2
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Body: Keep small UI state objects near the view and update them with plain Lua.

LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Name: LuaCard3
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Body: Use Lua for the parts that need logic, while XML continues to own the structure.

TextBlock.Name: MoonTitle
TextBlock.Text: MoonScript cuts down ceremony

TextBlock.Name: MoonBody
TextBlock.Text: MoonScript compiles to Lua, so app code can stay compact and expressive without changing the runtime story.

TextBlock.Name: MoonNote1
TextBlock.Text: • Good for larger app modules and controller code.

TextBlock.Name: MoonNote2
TextBlock.Text: • The syntax is terse, but the output remains plain Lua.

TextBlock.Name: MoonNote3
TextBlock.Text: • It fits Orca's split between declarative XML and runtime logic.

TextBlock.Name: MoonModelHeading
TextBlock.Text: Why it feels nice

TextBlock.Name: MoonModelLine1
TextBlock.Text: Less boilerplate around classes

TextBlock.Name: MoonModelLine2
TextBlock.Text: Readable app-level controllers

TextBlock.Name: MoonModelLine3
TextBlock.Text: Still ships as Lua under the hood

TextBlock.Name: MoonModelLine4
TextBlock.Text: Pairs well with XML screens and Lua helpers

TextBlock.Name: DeployTitle
TextBlock.Text: One UI, many targets

TextBlock.Name: DeployBody
TextBlock.Text: Build the screen once and keep the experience aligned across desktop, mobile, and web-style targets.

LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Name: DeployCard1
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Body: Keyboard and mouse friendly, with room for dense layouts and detailed information.

LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Name: DeployCard2
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Body: Touch-friendly spacing and responsive containers keep the UI comfortable on smaller screens.

LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Name: DeployCard3
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Body: The same app source can target browser workflows without changing the underlying screen model.

TextBlock.Name: DeployImage1Title
TextBlock.Text: City glow

TextBlock.Name: DeployImage2Title
TextBlock.Text: Night light

LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Name: CapabilityXml
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Title: XML first
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Body: Screens stay legible because layout lives in XML, not hidden inside a tangle of widget setup.

LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Name: CapabilityLua
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Title: Lua behavior
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Body: Use plain Lua for interactions, state, and the small bits of logic that make a UI feel alive.

LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Name: CapabilityMoon
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Body: MoonScript keeps larger app code concise while still compiling to the same Lua runtime.

LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Name: CapabilityShip
LayerPrefabPlaceholder.Card.Body: The same UI model can stretch across desktop, mobile, and web-oriented targets.

TextBlock.Name: IterationHeading
TextBlock.Text: A short build loop

TextBlock.Name: IterationSubheading
TextBlock.Text: Edit the XML, adjust the logic in Lua or MoonScript, and see the result without switching tools.

TextBlock.Name: IterationWriteBody
TextBlock.Text: Change layout, spacing, and structure where the screen lives.

TextBlock.Name: IterationRunBody
TextBlock.Text: Load the app and inspect the UI in context instead of guessing from mockups.

TextBlock.Name: IterationRefineBody
TextBlock.Text: Tweak assets, replace text, and polish the composition until it feels right.

TextBlock.Name: TargetsHeading
TextBlock.Text: Built to travel

TextBlock.Name: TargetsSubheading
TextBlock.Text: The same Orca app can be tuned for different screens while keeping one visual language.

TextBlock.Name: TargetDesktopBody
TextBlock.Text: Great for dense tools, editors, and dashboards with lots of information.

TextBlock.Name: TargetMobileBody
TextBlock.Text: Responsive grids and padding keep touch layouts readable and calm.

TextBlock.Name: TargetWebBody
TextBlock.Text: Ship the same experience into browser-oriented workflows without rewriting the whole screen.
